steps.title: Turn the slope into a second point
steps.content: The line has slope $-\frac{1}{3}$ and passes through $(9,5)$. Move $3$ units right and $1$ unit down to get another point on the line: $(12,4)$.

steps.title: Solve for the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content:
Use $y=mx+b$ with $m=-\frac{1}{3}$ and point $(9,5)$:
$$5=-\frac{1}{3}(9)+b$$
$$5=-3+b$$
$$b=8$$
The equation is $$y=-\frac{x}{3}+8.$$ This matches, so D is correct.
